title: Best Growth ETFs 2026: VUG, QQQ & Top Funds Compared
description: Compare the best growth ETFs of 2026 including VUG, QQQ, SCHG, and more. Analyze 3-year returns, expense ratios, and risk to find the top growth funds.

mainEntity.name: What is the best growth ETF to buy and hold?

acceptedAnswer.text: The best growth ETF depends on your goals, but popular choices include Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG) and Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ). VUG offers broader exposure to large-cap growth stocks with a very low expense ratio, while QQQ focuses heavily on the tech-heavy Nasdaq-100.

VUG and QQQ serve different purposes. VUG tracks the CRSP US Large Cap Growth Index and has a lower expense ratio (0.04%), providing broader diversification. QQQ tracks the Nasdaq-100, which has historically offered higher returns but with more volatility and a heavier concentration in the technology sector.

mainEntity.name: What is the highest returning growth ETF?

acceptedAnswer.text: Historically, technology-focused growth ETFs like QQQ (Invesco QQQ Trust) and SCHG (Schwab U.S. Large-Cap Growth ETF) have been among the highest returning over the last 5 to 10 years, driven largely by the outperformance of mega-cap tech companies.

mainEntity.name: Are growth ETFs a good investment?

acceptedAnswer.text: Growth ETFs can be excellent investments for long-term investors willing to accept higher volatility in exchange for potentially higher returns. They perform best during economic expansions and periods of low interest rates, but may underperform value stocks during economic downturns.

mainEntity.name: How do I choose a growth index fund?

acceptedAnswer.text: When choosing a growth index fund, compare the expense ratio (lower is better), the underlying index it tracks (e.g., Russell 1000 Growth vs. Nasdaq-100), the fund's historical performance, its top holdings to understand sector concentration, and its total assets under management (AUM) for liquidity.
